items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer (PolyShell variant)
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026, and CISA added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe released a regularly scheduled security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, resolving critical and important vulnerabilities. These could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution, including a stored XSS vulnerability (CVE-2025-47110) via server-side template injection.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source to protect against multiple vulnerabilities. These flaws could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and compromise store data.

items.id: PolyShell (no specific CVE/APSB provided, but related to APSB26-05)
items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered the PolyShell vulnerability, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store by disguising code as an image. Sansec observed attacks on 79.5% of all stores, with no official patch backported to older supported release lines.

items.title: Improper Access Control and Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
items.summary: Adobe Commerce versions 2.4.8-beta1, 2.4.7-p3, 2.4.6-p8, 2.4.5-p10, 2.4.4-p11 and earlier are affected by an Improper Access Control vulnerability leading to security feature bypass and a stored XSS vulnerability allowing malicious script injection. Exploitation of the XSS does not require user interaction and can lead to session takeover.

items.title: Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Codazon Magento Themes
items.summary: A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Codazon Magento Themes v1.1.0.0 to v2.4.7 allows attackers to execute arbitrary Javascript in a user's browser context via a crafted payload injected into the 'cat' parameter.

items.title: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') in Modern Minds Magento 2 WordPress Integration
items.summary: A stored XSS vulnerability exists in Modern Minds Magento 2 WordPress Integration m2wp, affecting versions up to and including 1.4.2.1. This flaw allows for improper neutralization of input during web page generation.

items.title: SessionReaper: Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution and Customer Account Takeover
items.summary: Discovered in August 2025, this critical flaw (CVSS 9.1) in all versions of Adobe Commerce and Magento allows customer account takeover and unauthenticated remote code execution under certain conditions. Adobe released an emergency fix in early September 2025, but mass exploitation attempts were observed by Sansec in October 2025.
